discovery.name: REVIEW_PERIOD_SUBMITTER
discovery.sql: SELECT rp.review_period_name AS REVIEW_PERIOD, TO_CHAR(rp.start_date,'YYYY/MM/DD') AS RP_START, TO_CHAR(rp.end_date,'YYYY/MM/DD') AS RP_END, sub.person_number AS SUBMITTER_PN, TO_CHAR(SYSDATE,'YYYY/MM/DD') AS RUN_DATE FROM (SELECT review_period_name, start_date, end_date FROM hrt_review_periods_vl WHERE status_code='A' AND SYSDATE BETWEEN start_date AND end_date AND review_period_name NOT LIKE 'Default%' ORDER BY start_date DESC FETCH FIRST 1 ROW ONLY) rp CROSS JOIN (SELECT p.person_number FROM per_all_people_f p WHERE SYSDATE BETWEEN p.effective_start_date AND p.effective_end_date AND REGEXP_LIKE(p.person_number,'^[0-9]+$') ORDER BY p.person_id ASC FETCH FIRST 1 ROW ONLY) sub

base_read._comment: Good goal plans reach base table HRG_GOAL_PLANS_B; the name lives in HRG_GOAL_PLANS_TL (US). Read by prefix on the goal plan name — unambiguous, no existing plan name starts with a numeric run prefix. GOAL_PLAN_ID present = created in base. The bad row (invalid GoalPlanTypeCode) never reaches base, so its name returns no row.
base_read.sql: SELECT tl.goal_plan_name AS GP_NAME, gp.goal_plan_id AS GOAL_PLAN_ID, gp.goal_plan_type_code AS TYP FROM hrg_goal_plans_b gp JOIN hrg_goal_plans_tl tl ON tl.goal_plan_id=gp.goal_plan_id AND tl.language='US' WHERE tl.goal_plan_name LIKE '${PREFIX}%'
